services.name: balance-dashboard-service
services.responsibility: Read-only aggregator for the Account Balance Comparison Dashboard. Aggregates one customer's eligible accounts (ACTIVE + SAVINGS/CURRENT/FIXED_DEPOSIT), ranks by balance DESC with accountId ASC tie-break (server-side, deterministic per ADR-004), caches the ranked result in Redis with 30s TTL, and emits one async audit event per request (cache hit or miss). No RDBMS state. No write operations. Defense-in-depth IDOR guard. Hexagonal layout: domain (Ranker, EligibilityPolicy, BalanceSnapshot, AccountView) + application (BalanceDashboardService use case) + infrastructure (AccountClientAdapter, BalanceCacheRepository, AuditEventPublisher).

services.name: account-service
services.responsibility: Existing — REUSED. Must add new batched endpoint GET /api/v1/accounts?customerId={uuid} returning List<AccountInfo> (all statuses, all types) — see SUBDEC-002 resolution in service-decomposition §4. balance-dashboard-service applies EligibilityPolicy locally.
services.extension: New endpoint: listAccountsByCustomer(customerId): List<AccountInfo>. Server-side filtering by status/type is the CALLER's responsibility.

services.name: audit-service
services.responsibility: Existing — REUSED with schema-only extension (no code change). Consumes AuditEventRecorded v2 (additive Avro fields per ADR-003). Inherits 7-year retention and append-only enforcement from money-transfer S3 ADR-012.
services.extension: AuditEventRecorded schema v1 → v2 (BACKWARD-compatible): 3 optional fields added with null defaults — purpose (string), cacheHit (boolean), accountCount (int). Code change required: NONE in consumer. DB change recommended: add 3 columns to audit_log (DevOps owns); fallback path is JSONB storage in existing payload column.

services.name: api-gateway
services.responsibility: Existing — REUSED. New route /api/v1/balance-dashboard/** → balance-dashboard-service (DevOps P2 config change only). JWT RS256 validation + X-Customer-Id injection from JWT.sub (existing pattern) is the first IDOR checkpoint.
services.extension: Route configuration only — no code change

services.name: identity-service
services.responsibility: Existing — REUSED unchanged. JWKS endpoint provides public key for RS256 JWT verification at api-gateway. Token issuance unchanged.

events.name: AuditEventRecorded
events.producer: balance-dashboard-service (NEW producer); transfer-service continues to emit v1 (backward-compatible coexistence)
events.outbox_justification: balance-dashboard-service has no RDBMS state for outbox to commit against (per ADR-001). Read-only audit emission. Accept at-most-one-event-loss-per-Kafka-outage in v1; revisit if compliance requires zero-loss.

decisions.title: Service boundary — new balance-dashboard-service vs. endpoint on account-service
decisions.decision: NEW balance-dashboard-service in the Accounts bounded context (CQRS-style read projection)
decisions.rationale: Clean SRP, independent deploy + rollback, isolated cache namespace, no blast-radius increase on account-service (which is on the critical path of money transfers). Reuse-first preserved: AccountClient + AccountInfo + auth filter + audit-lib + observability-lib + Resilience4j all reused. Extra scaffolding cost ~0.75 day vs Option A is worth the deploy isolation for demo day.
rejected_options: Endpoint on account-service — pollutes a critical-path service with feature-specific cache + Kafka producer; couples demo rollback; mixes responsibilities
Library auto-mounted into account-service — worst of both worlds; couples runtime without isolation benefit

decisions.title: Cache strategy — TTL-only for v1 vs event-driven invalidation
decisions.decision: TTL-only, 30 seconds, no active invalidation in v1. Event-driven invalidation deferred to v1.1 with documented re-open criteria.
decisions.rationale: Acceptable staleness ≤30s is appropriate for an informational read view (BR-013 staleness threshold 60s is the bigger UX signal). Event-driven invalidation needs customerId-from-accountId resolution which would require AccountDebited/AccountCredited Avro schema extension (cross-team coordination) or a new mapping table in BDS (contradicts ADR-001's no-RDBMS). Sweet spot 30s for hit ratio > 70% target.
rejected_options: Event-driven invalidation v1 — needs cross-service schema change + customerId resolution; +2 SP; schedule risk
5s TTL — kills cache hit ratio; constant cold-cache load

decisions.title: Audit event schema evolution — extend AuditEventRecorded v1→v2 vs new event type
decisions.decision: EXTEND AuditEventRecorded with 3 optional fields (purpose, cacheHit, accountCount) — BACKWARD-compatible Avro change. No audit-service code change required. Sequencing: DevOps registers v2 schema in Apicurio by D6 before BDS deploys.
decisions.rationale: Zero audit-service code change. Preserves single audit taxonomy across services (vs fragmenting into BalanceInquiryAudited / LoanInquiryAudited / ... in future). First-class fields (vs stuffing into existing payload map) enable cheap compliance queries over 7-year retention.
rejected_options: New BalanceInquiryAudited event type — requires audit-service code change; taxonomy fragmentation; topic proliferation
Stuff fields into existing payload map — loses type safety; bad compliance query ergonomics

decisions.title: Aggregation & ranking responsibility — server-side vs client-side
decisions.decision: Server-side ranking in balance-dashboard-service. Cache stores the pre-ranked, pre-filtered result. Response includes per-row rank field. FE does NOT re-sort.
decisions.rationale: Cache friendliness (warm hits skip rank computation), deterministic across clients (one Ranker comparator), ARIA aria-label='Account {rank} of {total}' is server-authoritative at first render (accessibility), single source of truth for accountCount in audit event.
rejected_options: Client-side sort — cache pollution, FE sort race risk for ARIA, breaks AC-001-H2 deterministic ordering contract
Hybrid (server stable sort + client balance sort) — worst of both

SUBDEC-001.question: FIXED_DEPOSIT balance = principal + accrued interest?
SUBDEC-001.resolution: CONFIRM BA default: principal + accrued interest as of balance_as_of. balance-dashboard-service is a thin pass-through aggregator and trusts AccountInfo.balance from account-service/ledger-service. If ledger returns principal-only, that becomes a ledger-service ADR — not the BDS scope. Tech Lead MUST verify AccountInfo.balance semantics for accountType=FIXED_DEPOSIT during OpenAPI authoring; if principal-only, escalate to PM/BA per SUBDEC-001 fallback rule.

SUBDEC-002.question: AccountClient.listAccountsByCustomer() single batched call exists?
SUBDEC-002.resolution: DOES NOT EXIST. Money-transfer's AccountClient only exposes getAccountInfo(accountId) per docs/artifacts/S5-backend-dev-money-transfer.json. Tech Lead MUST add: (a) new interface method listAccountsByCustomer(customerId): List<AccountInfo> to common-libs/account-client-lib, (b) matching GET /api/v1/accounts?customerId={uuid} endpoint on account-service (returns ALL accounts, all statuses, all types — caller applies eligibility filter). Resilience4j defaults specified: time-limiter 300ms, retry 2x exp on 5xx/IOException only, CB 50% / 100 calls / 30s open, bulkhead 20 concurrent.

SUBDEC-003.question: Existing audit-service event schema supports purpose + cacheHit?
SUBDEC-003.resolution: EXTEND, don't fork. AuditEventRecorded v1 → v2 with 3 optional fields (purpose, cacheHit, accountCount), BACKWARD-compatible Avro evolution. audit-service consumer code change: NONE. DB extension recommended (3 columns in audit_log) but JSONB fallback acceptable for demo. Sequencing: DevOps registers v2 in Apicurio by D6 before BDS deploys.
SUBDEC-003.owner_next: banking-devops (Apicurio registration) + audit-service owner (storage path decision by D5)

SUBDEC-004.question: Service boundary — new balance-dashboard-service or extend account-service?
SUBDEC-004.resolution: NEW balance-dashboard-service. Detailed trade-off analysis in ADR-001. Chosen for deploy isolation, clean cache namespace, SRP, no blast-radius increase on account-service. Extra ~0.75 day vs Option A pays back in operational separation for demo day.

nfr_traceability.performance_p95_lt_500ms_warm: Redis GET serves pre-ranked JSON; no upstream calls; audit publish off critical path (async fire-and-forget). Headroom ~420ms under 500ms. Ref: ADR-002, ADR-004, event-flows §4.
nfr_traceability.performance_p95_lt_800ms_cold: Single batched AccountClient call (SUBDEC-002), Resilience4j time-limiter 300ms; filter+rank ~5ms; cache-set + audit-publish parallel/non-blocking. Estimated ~390ms total. Ref: event-flows §5.
nfr_traceability.performance_audit_emit_overhead_lt_200ms: Kafka producer.send() fire-and-forget on IO thread; main response thread not blocked. acks=1 + idempotent producer. No outbox per event-flows §3.4. Ref: event-flows §3.3.
nfr_traceability.performance_accountclient_single_batched: TL adds listAccountsByCustomer endpoint per service-decomposition §4. BDS calls once, applies EligibilityPolicy locally.
nfr_traceability.performance_redis_cache_ttl_30s: SETEX with 30s TTL; cache-aside pattern. No active invalidation in v1. Ref: ADR-002.
nfr_traceability.availability_99_9_pct_bds: Min 2 replicas across 2 AZs; rolling deploys maxUnavailable=0; HPA on CPU 70%. Ref: service-decomposition §7.
nfr_traceability.availability_redis_fail_open: Catch Lettuce RedisException → log + metric cache_miss_reason=REDIS_UNAVAILABLE → fall through to AccountClient. No CB on Redis (fail-fast retries). Ref: event-flows §6.
nfr_traceability.resilience_circuit_breaker_accountclient: Resilience4j slidingWindow=100, failureRate=50%, open=30s. Aligns with money-transfer ADR-009.
nfr_traceability.resilience_retry_accountclient: Max 2 attempts, exp backoff (100ms, 200ms), retry on 5xx/IOException only. Idempotent GET safe to retry.
nfr_traceability.resilience_graceful_degradation: v1: isStale=true per row when now()-balanceAsOf>60s. v1.1 (deferred): last-known-good cache served with X-Cache:STALE when CB-open. v1 returns 503 on CB-open (demo simplicity). Ref: event-flows §7.
nfr_traceability.security_oauth2_jwt_rs256: Reused existing chain (api-gateway + identity-service from money-transfer). RS256 + JWKS.
nfr_traceability.security_idor_guard_defense_in_depth: (1) api-gateway injects X-Customer-Id from JWT.sub, strips client-supplied customerId; (2) BDS controller re-asserts; mismatch → HTTP 403 + audit FORBIDDEN. Endpoint takes NO customerId parameter by construction. Ref: architecture.md §4.1, event-flows §8.
nfr_traceability.security_account_number_masking: AccountInfo already carries masked accountNumber from account-service (money-transfer convention). Logback masking filter from observability-lib reused. Cache stores only masked. Ref: service-decomposition §2.
nfr_traceability.security_no_balance_in_logs: Logback JSON encoder excludes balance field; structured log fields: correlationId, customerId, cacheHit, accountCount, durationMs, result.
nfr_traceability.security_redis_encryption_at_rest: Cluster-default AES-256-GCM (consistent with money-transfer Redis usage).
nfr_traceability.security_feature_flag_disabled_in_non_st: @ConditionalOnProperty on controller bean; application-prod.yml sets balance-dashboard.enabled=false.
nfr_traceability.security_jwt_scope_accounts_read: @PreAuthorize hasAuthority SCOPE_accounts:read on controller. TL to confirm scope name (ASSUME-001).
nfr_traceability.compliance_pdpa_data_minimization: Response DTO carries only 8 fields (rank, accountId, maskedAccountNumber, accountType, balance, currency, balanceAsOf, isStale). No name/address/contact/openingDate.
nfr_traceability.compliance_pdpa_purpose_limitation: Every audit event carries purpose=balance-inquiry (new field per ADR-003).
nfr_traceability.compliance_pdpa_consent_reuse: banking-security performs early consent-coverage review at D2-D3 (RISK-003 mitigation).
nfr_traceability.compliance_bot_audit_on_every_retrieval: AuditEventPublisher.publish() called in BalanceDashboardService.loadDashboard() after every success AND on FORBIDDEN/ERROR. Cache layer NEVER short-circuits audit. Ref: event-flows §3.2.
nfr_traceability.compliance_bot_7yr_retention: Inherited from audit-service (money-transfer S3 ADR-012). v2 schema fields stored alongside v1.
nfr_traceability.compliance_bot_audit_for_excluded_accoun: balance_dashboard_excluded_accounts_total{reason} counter per excluded account; observable in Grafana for compliance audit.
nfr_traceability.accessibility_wcag_aa_lighthouse_90: FE concern — server contract supports it: per-row rank field (server-side ranking, ADR-004) enables FE to emit correct aria-label='Account {rank} of {total}' at first render.
nfr_traceability.observability_distributed_tracing: OTel SDK from observability-lib reused; traceparent propagated via HTTP + Kafka headers; spans cover gateway, controller, cache get/set, account-client, audit publish.
nfr_traceability.observability_structured_logs: Logback JSON encoder from observability-lib reused; required fields correlationId, customerId, cacheHit, accountCount, durationMs, result. NO balance, NO full accountNumber.
nfr_traceability.observability_metrics_prometheus: New metrics balance_dashboard_* namespace: requests_total, duration_ms, cache_hit_ratio, audit_events_total, excluded_accounts_total, cache_miss_reason_total. Ref: architecture.md §5.
nfr_traceability.observability_grafana_panel: DevOps P2 creates new dashboard using existing OTel/Prometheus pipeline. Panels: request rate, error rate, p95 latency, cache hit ratio, audit-event rate, excluded-account counter.
nfr_traceability.observability_alerts: AlertManager: error rate>1%/5min, p95>800ms warm-cache/5min, audit-event rate=0/2min (silent compliance failure).
nfr_traceability.scalability_50_peak_concurrent: Min 2 replicas, HPA on CPU 70% + custom metric balance_dashboard_requests_total rate > 30/s/pod.
nfr_traceability.scalability_horizontal_stateless: No session state, no in-memory cache; all state in Redis. HPA elastic.
nfr_traceability.scalability_accountclient_no_n_fanout: Single batched call per SUBDEC-002 / service-decomposition §4.

tech_choices.language_runtime: Java 21 + Spring Boot 3.x (matches money-transfer monorepo convention)
tech_choices.module_layout: backend/balance-dashboard-service/ in Maven monorepo per project-structure.md
tech_choices.hexagonal_layers: domain (Ranker, EligibilityPolicy, BalanceSnapshot, AccountView) + application (BalanceDashboardService) + infrastructure (AccountClientAdapter, BalanceCacheRepository, AuditEventPublisher) + interfaces/rest (BalanceDashboardController)
tech_choices.database: NONE — no RDBMS, no Flyway migrations. State is Redis cache + upstream account-service.
tech_choices.messaging: Apache Kafka 3.x (existing); topic audit.event-recorded; Apicurio schema registry v2 (per ADR-003)
tech_choices.cache: Redis 7 (shared cluster from money-transfer ASSUME-004); key prefix balance-dashboard:*; TTL 30s; Lettuce client with pool size 16/pod
tech_choices.auth: OAuth2/OIDC reused; RS256 JWT; scope accounts:read (TL to confirm name)
tech_choices.resilience: Resilience4j on AccountClient: time-limiter 300ms, retry 2x exp 100/200ms on 5xx/IOException, CB slidingWindow=100 failureRate=50% open=30s, bulkhead maxConcurrent=20/pod. NO CB on Redis (fail-open).
tech_choices.observability: Reused observability-lib from money-transfer: OTel SDK, Micrometer→Prometheus, Logback JSON with masking filter
tech_choices.deployment: Helm chart infra/helm/balance-dashboard-service/ (DevOps P1 scaffolds); min 2 replicas across 2 AZs; HPA on CPU + request rate; NetworkPolicy ingress from api-gateway only, egress to account-service+Redis+Kafka
tech_choices.feature_flag: balance-dashboard.enabled (Spring property), default false in non-staging

risks_for_tl.headline: SUBDEC-001 — FIXED_DEPOSIT may be principal-only from ledger
risks_for_tl.mitigation: TL verifies AccountInfo.balance semantics during OpenAPI authoring; if principal-only, escalate to PM/BA per fallback rule. NOT a blocker for scaffolding.

risks_for_tl.headline: Apicurio v2 schema must be registered before BDS deploy (ADR-003 sequencing)
risks_for_tl.mitigation: DevOps P1 registers v2 schema by D6. Staging consumer smoke-test on D7 before BDS deploys on D8.

risks_for_tl.headline: audit-service storage path for v2 fields — first-class columns vs JSONB
risks_for_tl.mitigation: DevOps + audit-service owner decide by D5. Demo can ship with JSONB fallback; column promotion can be a follow-up ADR.

risks_for_tl.headline: Lettuce pool size vs shared Redis maxclients
risks_for_tl.mitigation: TL verifies pool size 16/pod × 2 pods = 32 connections fits within shared cluster maxclients headroom. ASSUME-004 in PM register.

risks_for_tl.headline: Cold-cache p95 may be tight if account-service latency is higher than expected
risks_for_tl.mitigation: Time-limiter 300ms on AccountClient; QA P1 measures with realistic 10-account fixture by D3 (shift-left perf test). Contingency: document p95<800ms cold as v1 SLA, raise to 1000ms only if QA evidence shows it's needed (with BA sign-off).

open_items_for_tl: Confirm Resilience4j default values (timeout 300ms, retry 2x exp, CB 50%/100, bulkhead 20) by measuring against staging account-service latency
Confirm exact JWT scope name (accounts:read or alternative) against identity-service OAuth2 client config (ASSUME-001)
Confirm AccountInfo.balance semantics for FIXED_DEPOSIT during OpenAPI authoring (SUBDEC-001)
Decide audit-service storage path (first-class columns vs JSONB) with audit-service owner by D5 (ADR-003 §audit-service Storage Side)
Author OpenAPI spec backend/balance-dashboard-service/api/openapi.yaml with endpoint shape per service-decomposition §1.3
Add listAccountsByCustomer endpoint to account-service AND extend AccountClient interface in common-libs/account-client-lib (SUBDEC-002)
NO Flyway migrations for balance-dashboard-service — confirm by NOT scaffolding db/migration/ directory

metadata.notes: SA-001 initial. 1 NEW service (balance-dashboard-service) + 2 EXTENDED services (account-service adds batched endpoint, audit-service consumes extended Avro v2). 1 event (AuditEventRecorded v2, BACKWARD-compatible additive). 4 ADRs covering: (1) service boundary [SUBDEC-004 resolved], (2) cache strategy TTL-only v1, (3) audit event schema evolution [SUBDEC-003 / DEP-003 resolved], (4) server-side ranking. SUBDEC-001 confirmed (BA default, TL to verify in OpenAPI). SUBDEC-002 RESOLVED: new method listAccountsByCustomer required on AccountClient + account-service — specified in service-decomposition §4. All 8 NFR sections from BA nfr.md mapped to mechanisms. No shared-DB violation: balance-dashboard-service owns no RDBMS, only Redis namespace balance-dashboard:*. Demo-deadline discipline: event-driven invalidation, last-known-good cache, and outbox pattern all deferred with documented re-open criteria. Hexagonal layout target package layout specified for TL.

user_story_to_service_map.US-BC-002: balance-dashboard-service (response DTO carries balance_as_of + masked accountNumber + type)
user_story_to_service_map.US-BC-003: balance-dashboard-service + Redis cache + Resilience4j on AccountClient
user_story_to_service_map.US-BC-005: FE-only (server-side rank field in response supports ARIA — ADR-004)

subdec_resolution_summary.SUBDEC-001: confirmed BA default (TL verifies in OpenAPI)
